programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. programme.desc: The team suspects Gibbs's arch-nemesis is behind an explosion at a summit on global terror. Meanwhile, a grief-stricken Fornell struggles to deal with the fatal shooting of his ex-wife. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Joe Spano(n)

programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Revolting Rhymes
programme.desc: The second of two animations based on Roald Dahl's children's book, which puts the author's comic spin on the stories of Cinderella and Jack and the Beanstalk. Jack is madly in love with his next door neighbour Cindy - but after attending a ball, she only has eyes for a vain prince. He finds himself distracted from the pursuit of true love when a giant beanstalk starts growing at the bottom of his garden. The star-studded voice cast includes David Walliams and Rob Brydon as the ugly sisters(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The lovable lamb's plans for a fun day off turn into something far more exciting when he's forced to take a trip to the big city to save the farmer, who has lost his memory. Can Shaun and his friends return home safely following their adventure? Animation, featuring the voices of Justin Fletcher, John Sparkes and Omid Djalili(n)

programme.title: Inner Workings (2016)
programme.desc: Award winning animated Disney short that brings to life the constant internal struggle between head and heart(n)

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: A young boy orphaned by a car crash and left alone in a forest befriends a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley(n)

programme.title: The Archbishop of Canterbury's New Year Message
programme.desc: The Most Reverend Justin Welby looks ahead to the coming year and reflects on the importance of connecting with other people. He takes inspiration from a visit to Dover lifeboat station and the example set by the RNLI volunteers(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Spectacular moments from the series as cameras capture the extraordinary variety of life found in South America and visit Asia, the largest of all continents, that hides rarely seen creatures. The sometimes surprising cities of Europe are explored, as are the wilds of Africa, home to the greatest gatherings of animals. The searing heat of Australia boasts weird and wonderful wildlife, while North America is home to pioneering animals that make the most of every opportunity. Finally, there is a trip to the frozen wilderness of Antarctica(n)

programme.title: The Snail and the Whale
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's children's book. It tells the tale of a tiny snail who longs to see the world and hitches a ride on the tail of a huge humpback whale. Sally Hawkins and Rob Brydon head the voice cast as the title characters, with Cariad Lloyd and Diana Rigg(n)

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Marking a decade since the sitcom first appeared on screen, the cast of the award-winning comedy series reunite in a special show at the London Palladium. A whole host of treats and surprises are lined up as they look back over a decade of fun, friendship, romance and all that made millions of fans connect with this unique and very special sitcom. With Miranda at the helm, they also share some of their fondest memories of the show, along with a series of behind-the-scenes tales(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The time traveller returns for a fresh set of adventures, beginning with a two-part story. When intelligence agents around the world come under attack from alien forces, MI6 turns to the only people who can help - a certain Doctor and her companions. As they travel the globe in pursuit of answers, threats arrive from all sides, leaving Earth's security resting on the team's shoulders. But where will this planet-threatening conspiracy lead them? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star, with guest appearances from Stephen Fry and Lenny Henry(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the dust settles on another series of eventful New Year's Day celebrations, some of the neighbours are in contemplative mood, while for the rest, life will never be quite the same again. Will the next 12 months be any more kind to the long-suffering people of Walford?(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: In 1897, English lawyer Jonathan Harker travels to Transylvania to meet a new client, Count Dracula. Harker finds himself trapped in the ancient, terrifying castle, and it soon becomes clear that the Count himself is not living at all. Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss's adaptation of Bram Stoker's classic, starring Claes Bang, John Heffernan and Dolly Wells(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces highlights of the Premier League's New Year's Day fixtures, including Manchester City v Everton at the Etihad Stadium and Arsenal v Manchester United at the Emirates Stadium. Pep Guardiola's side had won their last four matches against the Toffees, including a 3-1 triumph in last season's corresponding game. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Chelsea at the Amex Stadium, Burnley v Aston Villa at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Leicester City at St James' Park, Norwich City v Crystal Palace at Carrow Road, Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur at St Mary's Stadium, Watford v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Bournemouth at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present some of the biggest hits of the past 12 months, including performances by Mabel, Lewis Capaldi, Blossoms, Dermot Kennedy, Freya Ridings, Celeste and Jack Savoretti. They also look back at some of 2019's most memorable music moments and celebrate the final number one of the decade(n)

Boxer Carl Frampton, journalist Victoria Fritz, dancer Wayne Sleep and TV presenter Rav Wilding take to the black chair, answering questions on The Office, the Chelsea Flower Show, theatrical producer Lilian Baylis and The Inbetweeners(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: After everything that has happened, Karen fails to get the answers she needs. A furious Mick confronts Linda when she wakes up hungover at the Vic. Martin worries when Sonia tells him he has been talking in his sleep. Kat and Mo are confused by Jean's sudden good mood. Honey reveals Adam has been in touch about new tenants - and when Mo reveals it's the Panesar brothers, Ash is stunned(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Russian ship the Demeter is a perfectly ordinary vessel, but her new voyage seems different. Not only does it include a motley collection of remarkable passengers, including one Count Dracula, but it also boasts a strange cargo - 50 boxes of mouldering Transylvanian soil. As the crew sets sail, they are soon locked in a life-or-death struggle to prevent the vampire reaching their destination of Whitby, England. Drama, starring Claes Bang, with Jonathan Aris, Sacha Dhawan, Nathan Stewart-Jarrett, Clive Russell and Catherine Schell(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting(n)

programme.title: Comic Relief: Here We Go Again
programme.desc: Lenny Henry takes a look back at the night of Comic Relief 2019, featuring celebrity-packed entertainment highlights, musical performances, comedy sketches and a chance to update on some of the people and projects who were featured. Highlights include Alan Partridge, Bodyguard, Top Gear and movie specials: One Red Nose Day and a Wedding and Mamma Mia - Here We Go Again(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Oldbury, West Midlands, to see a town house with a purple theme, while Martel Maxwell visits a plot of land in Greater Manchester and a dated semi in Heslington, Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers offer their views on a backpack that can be twisted round to the front for easy access, a rotating wrist torch, and a button-making set that requires no glue, scissors or needles. With Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Margherita Taylor meets a couple who have a £550,000 budget and dream of swapping coastal life in Southend-on-Sea, Essex for views of rolling countryside. She also visits Westonbirt, the National Arboretum, where the annual arboricultural show is in full swing, and sees tree experts putting a range of impressive skills into action(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore obtains three items from a tip in Surrey. Creative duo Josh and Oli are masters of woodwork, but will they be left scratching their heads when Sarah drops off a pile of old floorboards? Screen printer Daniel Heath hopes his bold designs will make a wooden tea trolley desirable again, and Sarah falls in love with a retro box of camera-developing equipment(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich use some unusual items to makeover gardens in Hoveringham and Coventry, with half an old boat and a tin air-raid shelter being transformed(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture restorer William Kirk takes on a cherished family rocking horse that is in desperate need of restoration. The paint work and plaster is flaking away and the leather work is tattered and torn, but saddler maker Suzie Fletcher is also on hand. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay works wonders on a Royal Doulton plaque, which was broken in two during a house move 57 years ago and has remained hidden in a drawer ever since. Plus, Brenton West works on a Victorian stereoscopic viewer. These were a popular form of entertainment for Victorians and make images appear in 3D for the viewer(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts a special edition celebrating 50 years of the quiz. She and regular team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson take a trip down memory lane as they are joined by three distinguished past guests in four-time Major-winning golfer Laura Davies, the most prolific try-scorer in English rugby league history Martin Offiah, and triple world champion gymnast Beth Tweddle, as well as former Rangers and Scotland striker Ally McCoist, who was once a team captain on the show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: More than one family is torn apart following the events of Christmas Day and despite Walford's biggest secret being exposed, an even bigger one has now formed. Only a handful of people know the truth - and only time will tell whether it will remain hidden(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a Second World War drama, rarely performed because of its historical inaccuracy and poor research. Director Chris plays Rufus Heal, a dashing pilot reduced to a desk job cracking German codes in a top secret Allied facility after losing his leg. He is assisted by Sandra as uptight Englishwoman Valerie Sky and Vanessa as French codebreaker Camille, under the watchful eye of Annie as Wing Commander Wickham. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Having successfully made landfall in Whitby, Count Dracula heads for the gleaming lights of London where he finds a new environment pulsing with fresh blood. He lays his plans to spread his foul vampire contagion, focusing his sights on Lucy Westenra, a beautiful and popular girl with no shortage of young men in her thrall. However, he finds his plans unexpectedly checked by an alliance of formidable foes. Claes Bang, John Heffernan, Dolly Wells, Joanna Scanlan and Morfydd Clark star in the final instalment of Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss' adaptation of the Bram Stoker classic(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The day of Godmother and Dad's wedding arrives and while Jake wants to know where Claire is, the Priest manages to upstage the bride. As secrets are revealed and decisions are made, will Fleabag find the ending she is looking for? Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson and Olivia Colman(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam explores Cornwall, visiting the Eden Project, Camborne Deaf club, and Land's Ends. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate the fake buffalo mozzarella being sold on supermarket pizzas, and counterfeit hearing aids that could make their wearers deaf(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond and a team of volunteers give the valais blacknose sheep a bath, Kirsty and Tom rescue fallen sheep on the isle, and Stevie attempts to milk his first buffalo. In Shetland, Kirsty and Tom again row out to St Ninian's Isle to attempt to rescue a ewe and a lamb that have fallen from the isle's steep cliffs(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts the semi-final, as the four remaining sewers take on garments and techniques from around the world. Firstly, they must follow a pattern like none they've encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a pair of Indian Dhoti Pants. Next is the Transformation Challenge, as the sewers take on a garment from West Africa, the Dashiki. For their final challenge, the Made to Measure, the semi-finalists take on the fiendishly tricky high-end Japanese couture technique of origami(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contest, hosted by Joe Lycett, reaches its final as the last three contestants take on a trio of evening wear challenges before the winner is announced. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young begin by asking the sewers to create a double-breasted waistcoat. Next is the Transformation challenge, as the trio repurpose net curtains into exquisite, red carpet-ready, pieces of evening wear. Lastly, for the Made to Measure challenge, the sewers attempt to construct and fit glamorous strapless evening gowns for their models, this time hand-picked for friends and family(n)

programme.title: New Year's Day Concert
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny presents this year's concert from the Golden Hall of the Musikverein in Vienna, led for the first time by celebrated Latvian conductor Andris Nelsons, who guides the Vienna Philharmonic in an array of polkas, waltzes and gallops by the Strauss family and their contemporaries. Furthermore, to celebrate the 250th birthday of Ludwig van Beethoven, the composer's Contretänze will be played, which will include a ballet scene in the television broadcast(n)

programme.title: The Thomas Crown Affair (1968)
programme.desc: A bored millionaire masterminds a bank robbery, only to find himself pursued by a glamorous insurance investigator determined to prove his guilt. It is not long before they get embroiled in a passionate affair - but conflicting interests mean they can never really trust one another. Crime drama, starring Steve McQueen, Faye Dunaway, Paul Burke and Yaphet Kotto(n)

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven (1960)
programme.desc: Oppressed Mexican villagers cross the border into America looking to buy guns to defend their homes and crops from bandits. Instead they encounter an enigmatic loner who persuades them to hire a band of gunslingers to teach the villagers how to fight before the marauders return. Western, with Yul Brynner, Steve McQueen, Charles Bronson, Robert Vaughn, James Coburn and Eli Wallach(n)

programme.title: The Archbishop of Canterbury's New Year Message
programme.desc: The Most Reverend Justin Welby looks ahead to the coming year and reflects on the importance of connecting with other people. He takes inspiration from a visit to Dover lifeboat station and the example set by the RNLI volunteers(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Romeo and Juliet: Beyond Words
programme.desc: Young stars of The Royal Ballet perform in a cinematic reimagining of Shakespeare's seminal love story, combining ballet, theatre, film and music. Shot on location in Hungary, the film features the iconic original costume designs by Nicholas Georgiadis, Kenneth MacMillan's remarkable choreography and Sergei Prokofiev's score performed by the Orchestra of the Royal Opera House, conducted by Koen Kessel(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Monica Galetti, Marcus Wareing and Gregg Wallace catch up with five of the competition's most successful past contestants, for whom taking part in the show has had a career-changing impact. Travelling to some inspiring and diverse gastronomic destinations, the judges find their past alumni making waves in the culinary world, then invite all the former finalists back to the MasterChef kitchen to demonstrate how much their food has evolved. In this spectacular rematch, there is extraordinary food and creativity on show, as the chefs battle to prove to the judges, once again, that they are best in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the quiz show as two former teams with a connection to drinks return for a happy hour special. The contestants must draw connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random, with one set of clues comprising Fuzzy, Stray dog, Trooper, and Mmmhops(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the first semi-final of the academic quiz's festive series. Hosted by Jeremy Paxman(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: David Harewood narrates a documentary exploring three of the most exotic and remote islands on the planet, beginning with the unique and extraordinary wildlife of Madagascar. As the oldest island on Earth, life has had time to evolve and there are now more unique plants and animals on Madagascar than any other, with footage of ring-tailed lemurs, labord's chameleons and Decken's sifakas(n)

programme.title: Dara O Briain - Voice of Reason
programme.desc: An exclusive chance to see the comedian Dara O Briain's Voice of Reason live show from 2019, filmed at London's Eventim Apollo. In the show, Dara addresses problems with virtual reality, having too much technology at home, how to deal with missing members of the front row and how to cope with reports of your own demise(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Life of a Year 2019
programme.desc: Rhys Thomas presents his annual satirical review of the year, offering a comical take on the year's big news stories, cultural events and most popular entertainment to create a spoof vision of 2019. Starring Christopher Eccleston, Lucy Montgomery and Tony Way(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The controversial comedian tries to make sense of the modern world, using a combination of stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction to review 2017. A panel of guests is on hand to take Frankie to task over his claims(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The outspoken comedian and his guests tackle some of 2018's biggest news stories, in a year that saw Brexit dominating the news once more, along with severe weather conditions and extreme politics(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian returns with his end of year special, with guests Sara Pascoe and Miles Jupp helping him dissect the bewildering year that was 2019. The show features a mix of Frankie's stand-up, reviews and discussion - all in an attempt to make sense of the last 12 months, which has seen Boris Johnson succeed Theresa May as prime minister, the further delay of the UK's exit from the European Union, the announcement of a general election and impeachment proceedings against President Donald Trump getting under way(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam explores Cornwall, visiting the Eden Project, Camborne Deaf club, and Land's Ends. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem (2016)
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the media-dubbed Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public. Into this melting pot is thrust well-intentioned inspector John Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. The Scotland Yard sleuth knows he has been set up to fail, but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno. He follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London. Thriller, starring Bill Nighy, Douglas Booth and Olivia Cooke(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Piper learns what life would be like if she never joined The Next Step(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Piper struggles to reignite the love of dance in everyone(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond's bull Major returns to work after being injured earlier in the year, but getting him to the field of cows involves a soggy detour. Raymond also travels to Switzerland with fiancee Jen to help their friends gather their valais blacknose sheep from the mountaintops. Kirsty and Aimee go sheep shopping to double their flock, while bad news forces buffalo farmer Stevie to make a shocking decision(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the latest heat, Monica Galetti challenges three of the chefs to employ deft knife skills to spatchcock a quail before cooking it with rocket pesto in just 15 minutes. The remaining contestants prepare Marcus Wareing's beignets with chocolate sauce, before all six get the chance to redeem any earlier mistakes when they cook their own signature dish for Monica, Marcus and Gregg Wallace. The judges then decide which three have done enough to make it through to the quarter-final stage of the competition(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: The 39 Steps (1935)
programme.desc: A Canadian in London offers a safe hiding place to a British secret agent investigating a spy ring. However, when she is murdered, he realises he will be the prime suspect and goes on the run with the intention of exposing the real killers. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller based on John Buchan's novel, starring Robert Donat, Madeleine Carroll, Lucie Mannheim, Godfrey Tearle and Peggy Ashcroft(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by pioneering film director Alfred Hitchcock, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look through the BBC archive to discover what makes an epic, featuring interviews with some of those who starred in some of the biggest film in the world. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: The Fall of the Roman Empire (1964)
programme.desc: Roman emperor Marcus Aurelius is poisoned by the henchmen of his mad son Commodus because he intends to hand power to the boy's more favoured and able adopted brother. But lacking sanity and authority, the usurper loses military control and Rome is overrun with barbarians. Historical drama, starring Alec Guinness, Christopher Plummer, James Mason, Sophia Loren, Anthony Quayle and Omar Sharif(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Famous faces embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge. Expert assistants help them unearth bargains at shops and markets, before they head to an auction to discover whose haul raises the most money(n)

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan joins pioneering biologist Dr Victor Lukarevskiy in Russia to attempt to rehabilitate two pairs of lynx held captive for years. Two of these cats, Koshka and Miass, were born in the wild, but have been locked in tiny steel cages for years by a fur trapper, while their Bryansk and Dasha have never seen the wilderness, and Dr Lukarevskiy wants to give these lynx a new life in the wild. Will they have the instincts needed to survive in the wild? And after years in human captivity, will they make it?(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as two former teams with ties to schooling compete, with recent graduates of Clare College Cambridge the Clareites taking on schoolmasters the Beaks. The contestants must draw connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the second semi-final of the academic quiz's festive series. Jeremy Paxman hosts(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: In the heart of south-east Asia lies the tropical island of Borneo. Twice the size of the British Isles, it is the third largest island on earth and home to possibly the greatest diversity of life of any island. Its huge variety of habitats, from bustling coral reefs and ancient jungles to towering mountains, have given rise to more than 60,000 species of plants and animals - many found nowhere else on Earth. This documentary covers Borneo's rich abundance of wildlife, from frogs to orang-utans(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Actress Amita Dhiri reflects on the year's pop-culture highs and lows, including her own role in hit BBC Two drama This Life. Spice Girls burst on to the music scene in a blaze of glory with number-one smash Wannabe, inventing girl power in the process, while Lara Croft got trigger-happy in the first instalment of the Tomb Raider game series. Mark Morrison waved his handcuffs to Return of the Mack, the Macarena was the latest dance craze and England lost to Germany once again in the Euro 96 semi-finals(n)

programme.title: Suits You Sir: The Inside Leg of The Fast Show
programme.desc: Behind-the-scenes documentary revealing how 1990s sketch show The Fast Show developed into one of TV's most successful series, charting how the characters were brought to life and the people who were a source of inspiration. With contributions from the creators, writers, cast and a few famous fans(n)

programme.title: The Fast Show
programme.desc: The gang roll out their favourite characters, including Jazz Club, Ted and Ralph, Jesse, Dave Angel, Swiss Toni, Competitive Dad and Suits You Sir, for the very last time. Guest starring Johnny Depp and Jodie Kidd, with Paul Whitehouse, Charlie Higson, Mark Williams, Arabella Weir and John Thomson(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Jason Bell and Osi Umenyiora to look back at the final round of regular-season fixtures. The trio also preview the upcoming play-offs(n)

programme.title: Operation Avalanche (2016)
programme.desc: Two young CIA agents are sent to NASA, under the false pretence of making a documentary, to investigate reports of a mole. Once there, though, they become entangled in a much bigger conspiracy involving the Apollo 11 mission and the filming techniques of director Stanley Kubrick. Thriller, starring Matt Johnson, Owen Williams and Andrew Appelle(n)

programme.title: The Case of Sally Challen
programme.desc: In 2010, Surrey mother-of-two Sally Challen bludgeoned her husband Richard to death as he sat eating lunch at the kitchen table, and was found guilty of murder at her trial in 2011. This documentary follows her appeal against the conviction, with her new defence lawyers arguing that she was the victim of Richard's coercive control, a newly understood type of psychological abuse, and that she should be in prison for manslaughter, not murder(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists head to Lancashire, where a mystery buyer wants a piece of art to mark a new time in their life, having changed career at 50, ended their marriage and suffered the death of their mother. A glass maker, a clay sculptor and a stone carver set out to make the mystery buyer's day on a budget of £600. Presented by Nick Knowles(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Thandi wants to uproot her family to New Zealand, but husband Theo isn't so sure. A trial week threatens to drive the two further apart. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Highlands, new entrant crofters Lynn and Sandra welcome more new sheep and take delivery of a batch of hens that they hope will lay the foundations of an 'egg empire'. In Northern Ireland, the Rodgers host a barbecue for the local Young Farmers Club, but it is no ordinary flame-grilled affair, while Raymond and Johnny gather the flock to wean this year's lambs in the Cairngorms(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs recreate Monica's razor clams with a shallot, caper and tomato sauce, before the second group of three take on Marcus' calves liver with a Bordelaise sauce. In the next round, the six chefs aim to impress the judges with a dish of their own design, with just one hour and 15 minutes to prepare it(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Rio Conchos (1964)
programme.desc: A shipment of rifles is stolen by a former Confederate general in the aftermath of the American Civil War, and he begins selling them to hostile Apaches. A cavalry major is assigned to bring back the weapons, aided by a mismatched band including one of his fellow officers, a duplicitous Mexican and a drunken former rebel. Western, starring Richard Boone and Stuart Whitman(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits archive interviews with some of Britain's biggest stars of the 1960s, including Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Peter Sellers, Peter O'Toole and Julie Christie(n)

programme.title: South Pacific (1958)
programme.desc: A naval nurse falls in love with a French plantation owner on an American-held island during the Second World War. However, dark secrets surface from his past and result in her having to leave the colony and embark on a perilous mission with the help of a US Navy officer. Romantic musical based on the Rodgers and Hammerstein Broadway show, with Mitzi Gaynor, Rossano Brazzi, John Kerr and Ray Walston(n)

programme.title: Snow Chick: A Penguin's Tale
programme.desc: Documentary following the first few months in the life of an emperor penguin born in an Antarctic midwinter. Kate Winslet narrates as cameras follow the young bird, watching him learn to survive the dangers of predators and the treacherous landscape of his home, before he is thrown into the ocean for the very first time(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Benidorm stars Sherrie Hewson and Tony Maudsley seek fortune in Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire. Providing guidance are dealer Roo Irvine and auctioneer Tim Medhurst. They'll finish at auction in North Hykeham, but first music lover Tony joins a merry band of toy soldiers in Melton Mowbray. Meanwhile, food lover Sherrie learns about one of the country's oldest biscuits - Grantham Gingerbreads(n)

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan and Dr Lukarevsky keep a close eye on Koshka's family as they continue on the road to full recovery. They have to prove their ability to hunt and survive before they can be released back to the wild. Bryansk has become so domesticated it's a major challenge for Gordon and Victor to help him find his wild instincts. If he cannot he will not be able to be released and will have to spend the rest of his life in captivity(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts a match between the winners of series 12 and 13, with the Verbivores taking on the Escapologists in the test of knowledge and lateral thinking as they battle it out to be crowned Champion of Champions(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as two teams each made up of four distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the final of the festive edition of the academic quiz(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig calls for a bit of 'Quiet', this time inviting Andrew Maxwell, Sara Pascoe and Jimmy Carr to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: In Search of Dracula with Mark Gatiss
programme.desc: Mark Gatiss explores and celebrates the icon of popular culture. He begins at Orava Castle in Slovakia, as used in the classic vampire film Nosferatu, then heads to the London Library on the trail of Bram Stoker's newly discovered research literature. In Philadelphia, he studies Stoker's hand-written notes and examines the abandoned ideas, plotlines and characters that went on to become this world-famous story. Mark also meets with actors, experts and historians as he explores the Count's transition from page to screen - and even manages to reunite seven Hammer brides. Contributors include the latest Dracula, Danish actor Claes Bang(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows (2014)
programme.desc: A documentary crew follows the daily lives of four vampires sharing a house in New Zealand as they prepare for an annual gathering of the undead. A new member of the household introduces the bloodsuckers to modern life, but his failure to follow the laws of their kind brings his flatmates nothing but trouble. Horror comedy, directed by and starring Jemaine Clement and Taika Waititi(n)

programme.title: New Year's Day Concert
programme.desc: Highlights of this year's concert from the Golden Hall of the Musikverein in Vienna, led for the first time by celebrated Latvian conductor Andris Nelsons, who guides the Vienna Philharmonic in an array of polkas, waltzes and gallops by the Strauss family and their contemporaries. Presented by Petroc Trelawny(n)

programme.title: The Ipcress File (1965)
programme.desc: An intelligence agent investigating the kidnap and brainwashing of Britain's most renowned scientists unpicks a complex chain of connections and high-level intrigue, while detecting evidence of double-dealing and counter-espionage. Spy thriller based on Len Deighton's novel, starring Michael Caine, Sue Lloyd, Gordon Jackson and Nigel Green(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: The life of acting legend Michael Caine, star of movies such as The Ipcress File (being shown next), The Italian Job, and The Dark Knight. Using rare TV interviews and classic archive clips, this programme tells the story of one of Britain's most successful actors. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: Goldsmith Alberto Larussa is found dead in his wheelchair, which he appears to have converted into an electric chair in order to commit suicide. However, Montalbano takes a different view of the case when he discovers that the dead man's will is a forgery - and suspicion immediately falls on Larussa's brother Giacomo, who was set to inherit everything. Meanwhile, Augello investigates the mysterious murder of an electrician. Italian detective drama, starring Luca Zingaretti(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: The first of four programmes exploring roots music in the US, beginning with the birth of country music and urban blues in the 1920s. As record sales rose in the early days of radio, companies began exploring rural areas in search of new artists. The programme features early recordings by Appalachian country music pioneers the Carter Family and Will Shade and The Memphis Jug Band, whose music prefigured modern rap and RnB. Narrated by Robert Redford(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night Guides
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock takes viewers on a journey in pursuit of comets and asteroids, from tales in ancient history to the cutting edge of modern astronomy. Throughout the programme, Maggie examines developments in astronomy and space exploration which have allowed scientists to understand more about these relics from the early days of the solar system(n)

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at TV appearances made over the years by Mary Poppins actress Julie Andrews, capturing the milestones of her life and career. Featuring interviews from the archives as well as classic clips. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: The death of a public works chairman and his wife in a car crash are initially ruled an accident, but Montalbano spots evidence of a more sinister explanation when he notes that the woman's fingernails are broken. Italian detective drama, based on the novels by Andrea Camilleri, starring Luca Zingaretti(n)

programme.title: Jonas Kaufmann: Tenor for the Ages
programme.desc: Documentary following the German opera singer over the course of two years, as he prepares to return to the stage after a vocal injury left him unable to perform for five months. The film captures his appearance at the 2015 Last Night of the Proms - when he was the first German to sing Rule, Britannia! - and his rehearsals for Verdi's Otello at Covent Garden(n)

programme.title: Daredevils and Divas: A Night at the Circus
programme.desc: A compilation of performances by circus troupes across the UK to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the first British circus, held on the banks of the River Thames. The programme is compered by Doug Francisco of the Invisible Circus and features modern and traditional acts, including inclusive circus Extraordinary Bodies, the Gandini Jugglers, Tweedy The Clown of Giffords Circus, Britain's biggest contemporary circus NoFit State, and two tightrope walkers from Cirque Bijou, who attempt a high-wire crossing of the River Wear(n)

programme.title: Searching for Sam: Adrian Dunbar on Samuel Beckett
programme.desc: Thirty years after the playwright's death in Paris at the age of 83, Line of Duty star Adrian Dunbar explores the life and work of Samuel Beckett to find out about the man behind Waiting for Godot. Although born and raised in Dublin, Ireland, he was a resident of Paris for most of his adult life, writing in both French and English, and was awarded the 1969 Nobel Prize in Literature for his work(n)

programme.title: A Hard Day's Night (1964)
programme.desc: The Beatles, harassed by their manager and Paul's grandfather, embark on a train journey to London for a TV show, but find themselves dogged by all manner of problems, including the inevitable overexcited fans. Musical comedy, starring the Fab Four alongside Wilfrid Brambell, Victor Spinetti and Anna Quayle. Songs include Can't Buy Me Love, Don't Bother Me and the title track(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A musical compilation, featuring mature rockers Tina Turner and Phil Collins, all-conquering funki dredds Soul II Soul, dance queen Paula Abdul, northern soul girl Lisa Stansfield and the stubborn indie pop of the Beautiful South. Madchester favourites Stone Roses and Happy Mondays put in an appearance, along with Stock Aitken Waterman stalwarts Kylie Minogue, Jason Donovan and Sonia. Plus, Sydney Youngblood, Mike and the Mechanics, Rebel MC and Double Trouble, along with duets from strange bedfellows Marc Almond and Gene Pitney and Linda Ronstadt and Aaron Neville(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's last full year as British prime minister contrasted with global political change, while in the music world Pete Waterman's final year of chart domination heralded the dawn of Madchester. Some female artists brought attitude and power to the year, while an unlikely duet between a '60s legend and an '80s icon proved a charttopper and Jive Bunny kept the less discerning happy. As far as Top of the Pops was concerned, Radio 1's old guard was stood down as a team of fresh-faced recruits helmed the show. Contributors include Jason Donovan, Lisa Stansfield, Shaun Ryder, Chris Rea, Marc Almond, Sharleen Spiteri, Jazzie B and Jenny Powell(n)

programme.title: Soft Cell: Say Hello, Wave Goodbye
programme.desc: Cameras follow singer Marc Almond and musician Dave Ball as they reunite for an emotional, sold-out, farewell concert at London's O2 arena in September 2018, marking the 40th anniversary of Soft Cell, one of the most colourful and charismatic bands in the history of popular music. Footage follows the build-up to the gig and provides an intimate retrospective portrait of the duo at work, as well as revisiting places of their youth. Clips from the concert itself are woven in with period archive and music videos(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure, lead singer of Ultravox and one of the men behind Band Aid, and Kim Appleby, who had a string of hits with her sister Mel in the Stock, Aitken & Waterman-produced band Mel and Kim, revisit the 1980s to determine why certain cities produced their own diverse tunes. In the first episode, they explore London's New Romantic movement, travel to Coventry to investigate the rise of Ska and speak to some of Sheffield's electronic music pioneers(n)

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog(n)

programme.desc: When Grampa Po moves to an upstairs flat, he misses the music played on the ground floor(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Science-educational series following a curious, adventure-seeking and unruly monster as he finds the answers to everything a pre-school child would want to know(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter discovers the dangers of babysitting when he is asked to watch over Jemima's egg(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch goes on a desert adventure to uncover a lost city, but is disappointed to learn someone else has found it already(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Goldilocks & the Three Bears
programme.desc: Join Mr Bloom and the Tiddlers as they accept an invitation to tea. Goldilocks and her Mum are waiting for their guests(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major gives three froglets medals for helping him, but the other froglets are jealous(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Eee gets separated from the Tombliboos Unn and Ooo, and needs Upsy Daisy's megaphone to find them(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Helen George reads a bedtime Story about a cat that has a special chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Revolting Rhymes
programme.desc: The second of two animations based on Roald Dahl's children's book, which puts the author's comic spin on the stories of Cinderella and Jack and the Beanstalk. Jack is madly in love with his next door neighbour Cindy - but after attending a ball, she only has eyes for a vain prince. He finds himself distracted from the pursuit of true love when a giant beanstalk starts growing at the bottom of his garden. The star-studded voice cast includes David Walliams and Rob Brydon as the ugly sisters(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The lovable lamb's plans for a fun day off turn into something far more exciting when he's forced to take a trip to the big city to save the farmer, who has lost his memory. Can Shaun and his friends return home safely following their adventure? Animation, featuring the voices of Justin Fletcher, John Sparkes and Omid Djalili(n)

programme.title: Inner Workings (2016)
programme.desc: Award winning animated Disney short that brings to life the constant internal struggle between head and heart(n)

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: A young boy orphaned by a car crash and left alone in a forest befriends a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: The Archbishop of Canterbury's New Year Message
programme.desc: The Most Reverend Justin Welby looks ahead to the coming year and reflects on the importance of connecting with other people. He takes inspiration from a visit to Dover lifeboat station and the example set by the RNLI volunteers(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Spectacular moments from the series as cameras capture the extraordinary variety of life found in South America and visit Asia, the largest of all continents, that hides rarely seen creatures. The sometimes surprising cities of Europe are explored, as are the wilds of Africa, home to the greatest gatherings of animals. The searing heat of Australia boasts weird and wonderful wildlife, while North America is home to pioneering animals that make the most of every opportunity. Finally, there is a trip to the frozen wilderness of Antarctica(n)

programme.title: The Snail and the Whale
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's children's book. It tells the tale of a tiny snail who longs to see the world and hitches a ride on the tail of a huge humpback whale. Sally Hawkins and Rob Brydon head the voice cast as the title characters, with Cariad Lloyd and Diana Rigg(n)

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Marking a decade since the sitcom first appeared on screen, the cast of the award-winning comedy series reunite in a special show at the London Palladium. A whole host of treats and surprises are lined up as they look back over a decade of fun, friendship, romance and all that made millions of fans connect with this unique and very special sitcom. With Miranda at the helm, they also share some of their fondest memories of the show, along with a series of behind-the-scenes tales(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The time traveller returns for a fresh set of adventures, beginning with a two-part story. When intelligence agents around the world come under attack from alien forces, MI6 turns to the only people who can help - a certain Doctor and her companions. As they travel the globe in pursuit of answers, threats arrive from all sides, leaving Earth's security resting on the team's shoulders. But where will this planet-threatening conspiracy lead them? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star, with guest appearances from Stephen Fry and Lenny Henry(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the dust settles on another series of eventful New Year's Day celebrations, some of the neighbours are in contemplative mood, while for the rest, life will never be quite the same again. Will the next 12 months be any more kind to the long-suffering people of Walford?(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: In 1897, English lawyer Jonathan Harker travels to Transylvania to meet a new client, Count Dracula. Harker finds himself trapped in the ancient, terrifying castle, and it soon becomes clear that the Count himself is not living at all. Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss's adaptation of Bram Stoker's classic, starring Claes Bang, John Heffernan and Dolly Wells(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces highlights of the Premier League's New Year's Day fixtures, including Manchester City v Everton at the Etihad Stadium and Arsenal v Manchester United at the Emirates Stadium. Pep Guardiola's side had won their last four matches against the Toffees, including a 3-1 triumph in last season's corresponding game. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Chelsea at the Amex Stadium, Burnley v Aston Villa at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Leicester City at St James' Park, Norwich City v Crystal Palace at Carrow Road, Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur at St Mary's Stadium, Watford v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Bournemouth at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present some of the biggest hits of the past 12 months, including performances by Mabel, Lewis Capaldi, Blossoms, Dermot Kennedy, Freya Ridings, Celeste and Jack Savoretti. They also look back at some of 2019's most memorable music moments and celebrate the final number one of the decade(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The vets operate on Violet the koala to investigate her breathing problems, and the keepers wonder which will come off worst when a tree porcupine comes face to face with a skunk(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Swadlincote, Derbyshire to see an ex-pub with potential. Dion Dublin looks around a house in Stoke, and the presenters revisit a project in Northwood, London(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Thandi wants to uproot her family to New Zealand, but husband Theo isn't so sure. A trial week threatens to drive the two further apart. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The consumers unbox, test and score a science kit, a pet seat carpet and a make-up cleanser and applicator. Lucy Alexander presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Gary Pe help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales, while host Charlie Ross tries his hand at coracle fishing(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of ex-pats who are raring to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore rescues colourful suede hides, a vintage radio and a set of microphone stands for transforming into potentially lucrative items(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers are in Stoke-on-Trent to help a young couple who have run out of ideas when it comes to their garden, having sorted their new-build home(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Resident clock expert Steven Fletcher sets out to restore an unusual time piece - a statue of a barmaid holding a swinging pendulum clock dial. Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with restoring a precious hand mirror that has lost its glass and incurred numerous dents, while specialist furniture restorer Graeme Mancha overhauls an iconic piece of contemporary furniture - an Eames chair(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out to discover the truth about love. It's a story of lonely hearts, stag nights and kissogram nuns, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Boxer Carl Frampton, journalist Victoria Fritz, dancer Wayne Sleep and TV presenter Rav Wilding take to the black chair, answering questions on The Office, the Chelsea Flower Show, theatrical producer Lilian Baylis and The Inbetweeners(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: After everything that has happened, Karen fails to get the answers she needs. A furious Mick confronts Linda when she wakes up hungover at the Vic. Martin worries when Sonia tells him he has been talking in his sleep. Kat and Mo are confused by Jean's sudden good mood. Honey reveals Adam has been in touch about new tenants - and when Mo reveals it's the Panesar brothers, Ash is stunned(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Russian ship the Demeter is a perfectly ordinary vessel, but her new voyage seems different. Not only does it include a motley collection of remarkable passengers, including one Count Dracula, but it also boasts a strange cargo - 50 boxes of mouldering Transylvanian soil. As the crew sets sail, they are soon locked in a life-or-death struggle to prevent the vampire reaching their destination of Whitby, England. Drama, starring Claes Bang, with Jonathan Aris, Sacha Dhawan, Nathan Stewart-Jarrett, Clive Russell and Catherine Schell(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting(n)

programme.title: Comic Relief: Here We Go Again
programme.desc: Lenny Henry takes a look back at the night of Comic Relief 2019, featuring celebrity-packed entertainment highlights, musical performances, comedy sketches and a chance to update on some of the people and projects who were featured. Highlights include Alan Partridge, Bodyguard, Top Gear and movie specials: One Red Nose Day and a Wedding and Mamma Mia - Here We Go Again(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Oldbury, West Midlands, to see a town house with a purple theme, while Martel Maxwell visits a plot of land in Greater Manchester and a dated semi in Heslington, Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers offer their views on a backpack that can be twisted round to the front for easy access, a rotating wrist torch, and a button-making set that requires no glue, scissors or needles. With Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Margherita Taylor meets a couple who have a £550,000 budget and dream of swapping coastal life in Southend-on-Sea, Essex for views of rolling countryside. She also visits Westonbirt, the National Arboretum, where the annual arboricultural show is in full swing, and sees tree experts putting a range of impressive skills into action(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore obtains three items from a tip in Surrey. Creative duo Josh and Oli are masters of woodwork, but will they be left scratching their heads when Sarah drops off a pile of old floorboards? Screen printer Daniel Heath hopes his bold designs will make a wooden tea trolley desirable again, and Sarah falls in love with a retro box of camera-developing equipment(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a zen space for a young couple longing for a Japanese-style garden. Working to a £7,000 budget, the designers attempt to bring a taste of the Orient to Blackburn, turning a mismatched garden into an area of tranquillity and serenity(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture restorer William Kirk takes on a cherished family rocking horse that is in desperate need of restoration. The paint work and plaster is flaking away and the leather work is tattered and torn, but saddler maker Suzie Fletcher is also on hand. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay works wonders on a Royal Doulton plaque, which was broken in two during a house move 57 years ago and has remained hidden in a drawer ever since. Plus, Brenton West works on a Victorian stereoscopic viewer. These were a popular form of entertainment for Victorians and make images appear in 3D for the viewer(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out to discover the truth about work. It's a story of bouncers, bin men and female wrestlers, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard feels the pressure as he designs and cooks a vegan spread for 160 wedding guests at a country estate house in south Wales. It's the bride and groom's special day and Matt must hit their exacting specifications, the standards of the venue and the expectations of the guests. With a cake and a selection of signature dishes to prepare, this tricky catering challenge has Matt in a spin(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: More than one family is torn apart following the events of Christmas Day and despite Walford's biggest secret being exposed, an even bigger one has now formed. Only a handful of people know the truth - and only time will tell whether it will remain hidden(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a Second World War drama, rarely performed because of its historical inaccuracy and poor research. Director Chris plays Rufus Heal, a dashing pilot reduced to a desk job cracking German codes in a top secret Allied facility after losing his leg. He is assisted by Sandra as uptight Englishwoman Valerie Sky and Vanessa as French codebreaker Camille, under the watchful eye of Annie as Wing Commander Wickham. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Having successfully made landfall in Whitby, Count Dracula heads for the gleaming lights of London where he finds a new environment pulsing with fresh blood. He lays his plans to spread his foul vampire contagion, focusing his sights on Lucy Westenra, a beautiful and popular girl with no shortage of young men in her thrall. However, he finds his plans unexpectedly checked by an alliance of formidable foes. Claes Bang, John Heffernan, Dolly Wells, Joanna Scanlan and Morfydd Clark star in the final instalment of Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss' adaptation of the Bram Stoker classic(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The day of Godmother and Dad's wedding arrives and while Jake wants to know where Claire is, the Priest manages to upstage the bride. As secrets are revealed and decisions are made, will Fleabag find the ending she is looking for? Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson and Olivia Colman(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Duncan MacLaren and Lorraine Millar from the NHS Lothian Spiritual Care team reflect on what they learn from their work with patients about what is important in life(n)
