programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Russian ship the Demeter is a perfectly ordinary vessel, but her new voyage seems different. Not only does it include a motley collection of remarkable passengers, including one Count Dracula, but it also boasts a strange cargo - 50 boxes of mouldering Transylvanian soil. As the crew sets sail, they are soon locked in a life-or-death struggle to prevent the vampire reaching their destination of Whitby, England. Drama, starring Claes Bang, with Jonathan Aris, Sacha Dhawan, Nathan Stewart-Jarrett, Clive Russell and Catherine Schell(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Serena finds her principles tested when she encounters the dealer who supplied drugs to her dead daughter, and is forced to make a life or death decision. Chloe finally feels ready to move forward in her life, but may have to destroy the hopes of a good friend to do so. Ange and Fletch prepare for their first New Year's Eve together, but Sacha and Dominic's interference could ruin their plans. Guest starring Charlie Condou (formerly Coronation Street's Marcus Dent)(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting(n)

programme.title: Comic Relief: Here We Go Again
programme.desc: Lenny Henry takes a look back at the night of Comic Relief 2019, featuring celebrity-packed entertainment highlights, musical performances, comedy sketches and a chance to update on some of the people and projects who were featured. Highlights include Alan Partridge, Bodyguard, Top Gear and movie specials: One Red Nose Day and a Wedding and Mamma Mia - Here We Go Again(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Oldbury, West Midlands, to see a town house with a purple theme, while Martel Maxwell visits a plot of land in Greater Manchester and a dated semi in Heslington, Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers offer their views on a backpack that can be twisted round to the front for easy access, a rotating wrist torch, and a button-making set that requires no glue, scissors or needles. With Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Margherita Taylor meets a couple who have a £550,000 budget and dream of swapping coastal life in Southend-on-Sea, Essex for views of rolling countryside. She also visits Westonbirt, the National Arboretum, where the annual arboricultural show is in full swing, and sees tree experts putting a range of impressive skills into action(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore obtains three items from a tip in Surrey. Creative duo Josh and Oli are masters of woodwork, but will they be left scratching their heads when Sarah drops off a pile of old floorboards? Screen printer Daniel Heath hopes his bold designs will make a wooden tea trolley desirable again, and Sarah falls in love with a retro box of camera-developing equipment(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich use some unusual items to makeover gardens in Hoveringham and Coventry, with half an old boat and a tin air-raid shelter being transformed(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture restorer William Kirk takes on a cherished family rocking horse that is in desperate need of restoration. The paint work and plaster is flaking away and the leather work is tattered and torn, but saddler maker Suzie Fletcher is also on hand. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay works wonders on a Royal Doulton plaque, which was broken in two during a house move 57 years ago and has remained hidden in a drawer ever since. Plus, Brenton West works on a Victorian stereoscopic viewer. These were a popular form of entertainment for Victorians and make images appear in 3D for the viewer(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts a special edition celebrating 50 years of the quiz. She and regular team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson take a trip down memory lane as they are joined by three distinguished past guests in four-time Major-winning golfer Laura Davies, the most prolific try-scorer in English rugby league history Martin Offiah, and triple world champion gymnast Beth Tweddle, as well as former Rangers and Scotland striker Ally McCoist, who was once a team captain on the show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: More than one family is torn apart following the events of Christmas Day and despite Walford's biggest secret being exposed, an even bigger one has now formed. Only a handful of people know the truth - and only time will tell whether it will remain hidden(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a Second World War drama, rarely performed because of its historical inaccuracy and poor research. Director Chris plays Rufus Heal, a dashing pilot reduced to a desk job cracking German codes in a top secret Allied facility after losing his leg. He is assisted by Sandra as uptight Englishwoman Valerie Sky and Vanessa as French codebreaker Camille, under the watchful eye of Annie as Wing Commander Wickham. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Having successfully made landfall in Whitby, Count Dracula heads for the gleaming lights of London where he finds a new environment pulsing with fresh blood. He lays his plans to spread his foul vampire contagion, focusing his sights on Lucy Westenra, a beautiful and popular girl with no shortage of young men in her thrall. However, he finds his plans unexpectedly checked by an alliance of formidable foes. Claes Bang, John Heffernan, Dolly Wells, Joanna Scanlan and Morfydd Clark star in the final instalment of Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss' adaptation of the Bram Stoker classic(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The day of Godmother and Dad's wedding arrives and while Jake wants to know where Claire is, the Priest manages to upstage the bride. As secrets are revealed and decisions are made, will Fleabag find the ending she is looking for? Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson and Olivia Colman(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Revolting Rhymes
programme.desc: The second of two animations based on Roald Dahl's children's book, which puts the author's comic spin on the stories of Cinderella and Jack and the Beanstalk. Jack is madly in love with his next door neighbour Cindy - but after attending a ball, she only has eyes for a vain prince. He finds himself distracted from the pursuit of true love when a giant beanstalk starts growing at the bottom of his garden. The star-studded voice cast includes David Walliams and Rob Brydon as the ugly sisters(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The lovable lamb's plans for a fun day off turn into something far more exciting when he's forced to take a trip to the big city to save the farmer, who has lost his memory. Can Shaun and his friends return home safely following their adventure? Animation, featuring the voices of Justin Fletcher, John Sparkes and Omid Djalili(n)

programme.title: Inner Workings (2016)
programme.desc: Award winning animated Disney short that brings to life the constant internal struggle between head and heart(n)

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: A young boy orphaned by a car crash and left alone in a forest befriends a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: The Archbishop of Canterbury's New Year Message
programme.desc: The Most Reverend Justin Welby looks ahead to the coming year and reflects on the importance of connecting with other people. He takes inspiration from a visit to Dover lifeboat station and the example set by the RNLI volunteers(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Spectacular moments from the series as cameras capture the extraordinary variety of life found in South America and visit Asia, the largest of all continents, that hides rarely seen creatures. The sometimes surprising cities of Europe are explored, as are the wilds of Africa, home to the greatest gatherings of animals. The searing heat of Australia boasts weird and wonderful wildlife, while North America is home to pioneering animals that make the most of every opportunity. Finally, there is a trip to the frozen wilderness of Antarctica(n)

programme.title: The Snail and the Whale
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's children's book. It tells the tale of a tiny snail who longs to see the world and hitches a ride on the tail of a huge humpback whale. Sally Hawkins and Rob Brydon head the voice cast as the title characters, with Cariad Lloyd and Diana Rigg(n)

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Marking a decade since the sitcom first appeared on screen, the cast of the award-winning comedy series reunite in a special show at the London Palladium. A whole host of treats and surprises are lined up as they look back over a decade of fun, friendship, romance and all that made millions of fans connect with this unique and very special sitcom. With Miranda at the helm, they also share some of their fondest memories of the show, along with a series of behind-the-scenes tales(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The time traveller returns for a fresh set of adventures, beginning with a two-part story. When intelligence agents around the world come under attack from alien forces, MI6 turns to the only people who can help - a certain Doctor and her companions. As they travel the globe in pursuit of answers, threats arrive from all sides, leaving Earth's security resting on the team's shoulders. But where will this planet-threatening conspiracy lead them? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star, with guest appearances from Stephen Fry and Lenny Henry(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the dust settles on another series of eventful New Year's Day celebrations, some of the neighbours are in contemplative mood, while for the rest, life will never be quite the same again. Will the next 12 months be any more kind to the long-suffering people of Walford?(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: In 1897, English lawyer Jonathan Harker travels to Transylvania to meet a new client, Count Dracula. Harker finds himself trapped in the ancient, terrifying castle, and it soon becomes clear that the Count himself is not living at all. Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss's adaptation of Bram Stoker's classic, starring Claes Bang, John Heffernan and Dolly Wells(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces highlights of the Premier League's New Year's Day fixtures, including Manchester City v Everton at the Etihad Stadium and Arsenal v Manchester United at the Emirates Stadium. Pep Guardiola's side had won their last four matches against the Toffees, including a 3-1 triumph in last season's corresponding game. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Chelsea at the Amex Stadium, Burnley v Aston Villa at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Leicester City at St James' Park, Norwich City v Crystal Palace at Carrow Road, Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur at St Mary's Stadium, Watford v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Bournemouth at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present some of the biggest hits of the past 12 months, including performances by Mabel, Lewis Capaldi, Blossoms, Dermot Kennedy, Freya Ridings, Celeste and Jack Savoretti. They also look back at some of 2019's most memorable music moments and celebrate the final number one of the decade(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The vets operate on Violet the koala to investigate her breathing problems, and the keepers wonder which will come off worst when a tree porcupine comes face to face with a skunk(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Swadlincote, Derbyshire to see an ex-pub with potential. Dion Dublin looks around a house in Stoke, and the presenters revisit a project in Northwood, London(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Thandi wants to uproot her family to New Zealand, but husband Theo isn't so sure. A trial week threatens to drive the two further apart. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The consumers unbox, test and score a science kit, a pet seat carpet and a make-up cleanser and applicator. Lucy Alexander presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Gary Pe help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales, while host Charlie Ross tries his hand at coracle fishing(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of ex-pats who are raring to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore rescues colourful suede hides, a vintage radio and a set of microphone stands for transforming into potentially lucrative items(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist a woman in Hampshire, whose front garden is out of control, with lots of messy shrubs and a front door the postman cannot find. They also help animal lover Sue in Lymington, who would like to share her garden with the hedgehogs that live in the neighbourhood(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Resident clock expert Steven Fletcher sets out to restore an unusual time piece - a statue of a barmaid holding a swinging pendulum clock dial. Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with restoring a precious hand mirror that has lost its glass and incurred numerous dents, while specialist furniture restorer Graeme Mancha overhauls an iconic piece of contemporary furniture - an Eames chair(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Boxer Carl Frampton, journalist Victoria Fritz, dancer Wayne Sleep and TV presenter Rav Wilding take to the black chair, answering questions on The Office, the Chelsea Flower Show, theatrical producer Lilian Baylis and The Inbetweeners(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: After everything that has happened, Karen fails to get the answers she needs. A furious Mick confronts Linda when she wakes up hungover at the Vic. Martin worries when Sonia tells him he has been talking in his sleep. Kat and Mo are confused by Jean's sudden good mood. Honey reveals Adam has been in touch about new tenants - and when Mo reveals it's the Panesar brothers, Ash is stunned(n)

programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson and Olivia Colman(n)

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: The first Duke of Wellington - victor at the Battle of Waterloo - was a military hero who served twice as Prime Minister. Carolyn Quinn meets the current Duke for a tour of the family's London home, Apsley House(n)

programme.title: The Political Studies Association Awards
programme.desc: Highlights from the Political Studies Association Annual Awards cerenomy in London, from Monday 11 November(n)

programme.title: Reflections: When Parties Split
programme.desc: In the first programme in a series of half-hour unscripted talks on party splits, political journalist Steve Richards looks at Robert Peel and the Corn Laws(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to novelist and politician Jeffrey Archer about new book 'Nothing Ventured'(n)

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. See the teenage Bercow's ‘Question Time' debut, not from the Commons but on the TV programme. Relive the former Tory MP's clashes with ministers as he's accused of bias and of bending the rules to try to stop Brexit. In addition - with more Commons archive - Bercow Bows Out offers the chance to re-live some of his favourite unique phrases, whether he's advising an MP to search for ‘a soothing medicament' or accusing others of ‘chuntering from a sedentary position'(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption was until recently a justice of the UK's Supreme Court, as well as being a distinguished historian. He argues that the law is taking over the space once occupied by politics. Presented and chaired by Anita Anand(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Highlights from The Queen's Speech debate in the House of Commons, including speeches by Prime Minister Boris Johnson, Leader of the Opposition Jeremy Corbyn and the SNP leader at Westminster Ian Blackford, from Thursday 19 December(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to author Jack Brown about his book 'No.10: The Geography of Power at Downing Street(n)

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Historian Dr Philip Salmon tours Knowsley Hall, ancestral home of the 14th Earl of Derby, who was three times prime minister in the Victorian era(n)

programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. See the teenage Bercow's ‘Question Time' debut, not from the Commons but on the TV programme. Relive the former Tory MP's clashes with ministers as he's accused of bias and of bending the rules to try to stop Brexit. In addition - with more Commons archive - Bercow Bows Out offers the chance to re-live some of his favourite unique phrases, whether he's advising an MP to search for ‘a soothing medicament' or accusing others of ‘chuntering from a sedentary position'(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption was until recently a justice of the UK's Supreme Court, as well as being a distinguished historian. He argues that the law is taking over the space once occupied by politics. Presented and chaired by Anita Anand(n)

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Walks: Top 100
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Ore Oduba present a countdown of the UK's best loved walks, voted for by more than 8,000 enthusiasts. Featuring locations from the West Highland Way to the Ridgeway, and almost everywhere in between. Famous faces including Catherine Tyldesley, Adrian Edmondson, Katherine Kelly, Larry Lamb, Janet Street Porter and Robert Bathurst hit the trails to reveal their own favourite walks, which feature in the poll. programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino's train journey takes him on a star-studded line-up of Tuscan cities. He begins in Pisa, famous for its leaning tower, and also home of the Vespa scooter, which gives the chef the ideal way to explore the city, before he hops back on the train toward the medieval city of Lucca, the birthplace of composer Puccini. Here, he meets master violin-maker Fabio and rustles up a vegetarian asparagus dish. His final stop is Florence, where he meets Cecilia, one of a network of local cooks keeping traditional recipes alive by opening up her kitchen to guests from all over the world(n)

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Walks: Top 100
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Ore Oduba present a countdown of the UK's best loved walks, voted for by more than 8,000 enthusiasts. Featuring locations from the West Highland Way to the Ridgeway, and almost everywhere in between. Famous faces including Catherine Tyldesley, Adrian Edmondson, Katherine Kelly, Larry Lamb, Janet Street Porter and Robert Bathurst hit the trails to reveal their own favourite walks, which feature in the poll. They are joined by experts, nature aficionados and members of the public who provide an insight into the hikes, the history and the hidden stories behind the trails(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: The programme explores what makes this time of the year so special in Britain's countryside. Ben Fogle climbs Helvellyn with a man who battles the elements in the Lake District every day, while Paul Heiney views the Peak District from a glider and Liz Bonnin witnesses thousands of swans begin their journey from the fens of Cambridgeshire to their winter home in Russia(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino's train journey takes him on a star-studded line-up of Tuscan cities. He begins in Pisa, famous for its leaning tower, and also home of the Vespa scooter, which gives the chef the ideal way to explore the city, before he hops back on the train toward the medieval city of Lucca, the birthplace of composer Puccini. Here, he meets master violin-maker Fabio and rustles up a vegetarian asparagus dish. His final stop is Florence, where he meets Cecilia, one of a network of local cooks keeping traditional recipes alive by opening up her kitchen to guests from all over the world(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury

The presenter is joined by celebrated mountaineer Chris Bonington, to whom she asks - which is better - Everest or Castle Crag? programme.title: Spy Kids 3-D: Game Over (2003)
programme.desc: Young agent Juni has to go it alone when his big sister Carmen is kidnapped by the evil Toymaker, forcing the plucky junior spy to infiltrate the villain's virtual reality world to rescue her. Adventure sequel, starring Daryl Sabara, Alexa Vega, Sylvester Stallone, Antonio Banderas, Ricardo Montalban, George Clooney, Steve Buscemi, Salma Hayek and Elijah Wood(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: While Diane worries about what lies in store, Leyla's attraction to Liam grows, and Kim is glad of a distraction(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jai catches Al in the act, Leyla is unsure where she stands, and Moira is thrown into panic(n)

programme.title: The Big Quiz
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a titanic clash between ITV's biggest soaps. Headed by team captains Jack P Shepherd (David Platt) and Mark Charnock (Marlon Dingle), tensions run high as the casts of Coronation Street and Emmerdale battle it out to find out who knows their soap best. Sally Ann Matthews, Victoria Ekanoye, Tony Audenshaw and Sandra Marvin make up the sides(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Magician Shin Lim joins the host to talk about his Las Vegas residency show called Limitless. Plus, comedian Rhea Butcher performs a stand-up routine(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Joel Dommett takes a look back at the most outrageous, bizarre and jaw-dropping moments the jungle has ever seen, including highlights from this year's series. Plus, with a little help from a host of legendary ex-campmates Joel is reminded of just how crazy life gets when Jungle Fever sets in(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Just a few days after the new King or Queen of the jungle was crowned, Ant and Dec present the usual round-up of what happened behind the scenes as each campmate was voted out. From the first to the last, the programme reveals what they get up to in their first few days of freedom, and captures their reactions to old rivals and trusted friends, while their families gather for emotional reunions with their loved ones(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents a one-off special of the dating game, hoping to help 30 single women who work in the emergency services find a date on the Isle of Fernandos. Entering the love lift are firefighter John, doctor Iman and paramedic Johnny(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: Jurassic Park III (2001)
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Dr Alan Grant is lured to a dinosaur-infested island by a divorced couple who offer to fund his research, unaware their real motive is to find their missing son - and that the creatures on the island are far more intelligent than anyone suspected. Action adventure sequel, starring Sam Neill, William H Macy, Tea Leoni and Alessandro Nivola(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Jurassic Park III (2001)
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Dr Alan Grant is lured to a dinosaur-infested island by a divorced couple who offer to fund his research, unaware their real motive is to find their missing son - and that the creatures on the island are far more intelligent than anyone suspected. Action adventure sequel, starring Sam Neill, William H Macy, Tea Leoni and Alessandro Nivola(n)

programme.title: Pitch Perfect (2012)
programme.desc: A new student finds herself a social outcast during her first year of college, but soon acquires a new circle of friends in a band of misfits with a shared talent for singing. She joins their a cappella group and leads them to compete in the campus music competitions. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Rebel Wilson and Elizabeth Banks(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Pitch Perfect (2012)
programme.desc: A new student finds herself a social outcast during her first year of college, but soon acquires a new circle of friends in a band of misfits with a shared talent for singing. She joins their a cappella group and leads them to compete in the campus music competitions. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Rebel Wilson and Elizabeth Banks(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Jean announces that Casualty may have to close in light of a financial package to repair the department being rejected. With no admissions or emergency surgery, a round of cost-cutting redundancies seems inevitable - until a mystery benefactor offers to help. When an unexploded wartime bomb detonates, Jill and Ralph have to operate on a tramp trapped in the wreckage of a collapsed buildinge. Guest starring George Irving(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor Mildred's hopes are raised when work-shy George looks for a job, but his chances of finding gainful employment seem slim. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The couple offer their services to a woman in labour, despite their lack of experience - though George does know how to dismantle a motorbike. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: The Secret Garden (1993)
programme.desc: An orphan is sent to live with her uncle in his forbidding Yorkshire mansion, where she meets her bedridden cousin - a boy she knew nothing about. She also stumbles across a marvellous but neglected garden, and sets about nursing them both back to health, with the help of a lad from a nearby village. Children's drama, based on Frances Hodgson Burnett's book, with Kate Maberly, Heydon Prowse, Andrew Knott, Maggie Smith and John Lynch(n)

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan(n)

programme.title: Goodbye Mr Chips
programme.desc: Martin Clunes stars as an earnest Latin master working at the Brookfield School of the 1880s, where he meets and marries his true love Kathie and finds himself touched by politics, grief and the utter devastation of World War One. Charting more than 50 years of one man's extraordinary life, the story ends in the 1930s, as a wise, elderly and much-loved Mr Chipping lives out his last days in retirement. Frank Delaney's adaptation of James Hilton's timeless classic, which has never been out of print since first appearing as a magazine serialisation in 1934, and for which Robert Donat whipped an Oscar from under the nose of Gone with the Wind's Clark Gable in the 1939 film production. Also starring Victoria Hamilton, Conleth Hill and Patrick Malahide(n)

programme.title: Carry On Matron (1972)
programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: The Royal Variety Performance
programme.desc: Rob Beckett and Romesh Ranganathan host the annual entertainment extravaganza from the London Palladium, attended by the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge. The show features music from Robbie Williams, Jamie Cullum, Emeli Sande, Lewis Capaldi, the Kanneh Masons and Mabel, as well as this year's Britain's Got Talent winner, Colin Thackery, and comedy from Frank Skinner, Kerry Godliman and Flo & Joan. There are also appearances by the casts of West End shows Groan Ups, Mary Poppins and Come from Away, and dance spectacle from Zurcaroh and Cirque du Soleil(n)

programme.title: Elton John: The Nation's Favourite Song
programme.desc: A countdown of the singer's 20 most popular songs, chosen by viewers to celebrate his 70th birthday. Elton discusses the stories behind the tracks in a candid conversation with David Walliams, while celebrity fans and friends reveal what their favourite numbers mean to them. With contributions from Ed Sheeran, Annie Lennox, Stephen Fry and Elton's long-time songwriting partner Bernie Taupin(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. Vera discovers that the dead woman had been working for the prison service re-investigating a crime that a former prisoner had been convicted of, and realises she must finish what the victim started in order to discover who may have wanted her dead. Paul Kaye plays a new pathologist alongside fellow guest star Peter Davison(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan smuggles some tools out of work to help Arthur to get his motorcycle working(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Ormerod opens a can of worms while attending to the problems of a local family, and when violence spills on to the wards he is caught in the crossfire. Meanwhile, Aisling's adoption is official, but Sister Brigid is confused by her feelings. Last-ever episode of the hospital drama, starring Robert Daws, Linda Armstrong, Amy Robbins and Glynis Barber(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George decides to place his father in a residential home, but the old man would rather stay with his son. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star(n)

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott(n)

programme.title: Carry On Behind (1975)
programme.desc: Professors Ronald Crump and Anna Vooshka embark on an archaeological dig, but find their efforts disrupted by the saucy shenanigans of a motley bunch of holiday-makers in the neighbouring caravan park. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Elke Sommer, Joan Sims, Windsor Davies, Kenneth Connor and Bernard Bresslaw(n)

programme.title: Holiday on the Buses (1973)
programme.desc: Bus driver Stan and conductor Jack are fired and wind up working at a holiday camp in Wales, where they fall foul of the security officer - none other than their old adversary Inspector Blake, who has also lost his job and been forced into alternative employment. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant, Stephen Lewis, Anna Karen and Doris Hare(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local man confesses to a girl's murder of 11 years previously, prompting Sgt Miller to reopen the case. A search fails to uncover the victim's body, but the investigation takes an intriguing twist when PC Younger unearths a male skeleton instead. Meanwhile, Peggy sets her sights on a wealthy widower. Rural drama, guest starring Graham Turner and Geoffrey Hutchings, with John Duttine, Steven Blakeley and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The embers of a romance turn into flames for Pollard. Chris's pride comes before a fall. Jessica's news alarms Biff(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Michael's behaviour worries Rachel, while Viv's sister brings a warning. Zoe decides to give Chris a chance. Jessica is deeply unimpressed by Bernard's invitation(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is proud when Jack's story appears in a national paper. Liz worries about Steve's whereabouts. Bet tries to forget Charlie by going on a shopping spree(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre books a holiday in Morocco to get away from the Street. Des cannot bring himself to tell Raquel about Tanya. Curly is forced into letting Steve stay at number seven(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local man confesses to a girl's murder of 11 years previously, prompting Sgt Miller to reopen the case. A search fails to uncover the victim's body, but the investigation takes an intriguing twist when PC Younger unearths a male skeleton instead. Meanwhile, Peggy sets her sights on a wealthy widower. Rural drama, guest starring Graham Turner and Geoffrey Hutchings, with John Duttine, Steven Blakeley and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is proud when Jack's story appears in a national paper. Liz worries about Steve's whereabouts. Bet tries to forget Charlie by going on a shopping spree(n)

programme.desc: Deirdre books a holiday in Morocco to get away from the Street. Des cannot bring himself to tell Raquel about Tanya. Curly is forced into letting Steve stay at number seven(n)

programme.desc: The embers of a romance turn into flames for Pollard. Chris's pride comes before a fall. Jessica's news alarms Biff(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Michael's behaviour worries Rachel, while Viv's sister brings a warning. Zoe decides to give Chris a chance. Jessica is deeply unimpressed by Bernard's invitation(n)

Rural drama, guest starring Graham Turner and Geoffrey Hutchings, with John Duttine, Steven Blakeley and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp (1980)
programme.desc: Seedy landlord Rigsby takes in a seemingly respectable new lodger who jeopardises his efforts to romance tenant Miss Jones - but the new arrival may not be all he appears. Meanwhile, the addition of a rugby playing art student to the house sparks a fitness craze that leads Rigsby to enter a boxing match. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A group of young offenders come to stay at Ashfordly Hall, but soon find themselves under suspicion after a spate of disturbances in the village. Meanwhile, Blaketon and Ventress go on the hunt for a spinster's missing dog, and a £50 reward gives Peggy an idea. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, William Simons, Derek Fowlds and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv has some unwelcome news for Vic. The Sugdens name the day and Bernard has plans for the village's future. Betty lays the law down to Seth. Jessica has to face up to her future(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic runs into difficulties on the moors. Biff shows his true colours. Lynn makes Jack an offer, while Seth's new living arrangements cause speculation(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack tries to hide the paper from Vera when news of his affair breaks. Maud tells Percy about Maureen's real father. Deirdre leaves for Morocco(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily supports Percy over finishing with Maud. The Websters decide to have a holiday in Blackpool. Jack's attempts to cook a nice meal for Vera backfire spectacularly(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A group of young offenders come to stay at Ashfordly Hall, but soon find themselves under suspicion after a spate of disturbances in the village. Meanwhile, Blaketon and Ventress go on the hunt for a spinster's missing dog, and a £50 reward gives Peggy an idea. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, William Simons, Derek Fowlds and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Jean announces that Casualty may have to close in light of a financial package to repair the department being rejected. With no admissions or emergency surgery, a round of cost-cutting redundancies seems inevitable - until a mystery benefactor offers to help. When an unexploded wartime bomb detonates, Jill and Ralph have to operate on a tramp trapped in the wreckage of a collapsed buildinge. Guest starring George Irving(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor Mildred's hopes are raised when work-shy George looks for a job, but his chances of finding gainful employment seem slim. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The couple offer their services to a woman in labour, despite their lack of experience - though George does know how to dismantle a motorbike. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.desc: Seedy landlord Rigsby takes in a seemingly respectable new lodger who jeopardises his efforts to romance tenant Miss Jones - but the new arrival may not be all he appears. Meanwhile, the addition of a rugby playing art student to the house sparks a fitness craze that leads Rigsby to enter a boxing match. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A group of young offenders come to stay at Ashfordly Hall, but soon find themselves under suspicion after a spate of disturbances in the village. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, William Simons, Derek Fowlds and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden(n)

Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song(n)

programme.title: Vera

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade continues his investigations into supposedly deadly freshwater creatures. From right across the Amazon there are stories of a colossal fish that is said to swallow men whole. Some call it the 'lau-lau', others the 'piraiba'. But after 20 years of criss-crossing South America, Jeremy has never seen a specimen big enough to convince him that the fishermen's tales are true. In this programme, he heads to a remote and little-known backwater of Guyana on a mission to finally track down this alleged monster(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Hundreds died when the Sobral Santos II capsized not far from the shores of the Amazon in September 1981, and many of the stricken passengers were reportedly dragged to their deaths by powerful unseen attackers. Freshwater detective Jeremy Wade attempts to identify the culprits that played a part in the river's worst-ever boating disaster, but has the trail gone cold after more than 30 years?(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade continues his investigations into supposedly deadly freshwater creatures. From right across the Amazon there are stories of a colossal fish that is said to swallow men whole. Some call it the 'lau-lau', others the 'piraiba'. But after 20 years of criss-crossing South America, Jeremy has never seen a specimen big enough to convince him that the fishermen's tales are true. In this programme, he heads to a remote and little-known backwater of Guyana on a mission to finally track down this alleged monster(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Hundreds died when the Sobral Santos II capsized not far from the shores of the Amazon in September 1981, and many of the stricken passengers were reportedly dragged to their deaths by powerful unseen attackers. programme.desc: Documentary following the progress of artistic director Ake Larson and architect Arne Bergh as they embark on a six-week project to build Icehotel, a sophisticated structure constructed in the Swedish village of Jukkasjarvi - more than 120 miles inside the Arctic Circle. The venue has been built from snow and ice every year since 1990, when an edifice housing an exhibition of ice sculptures became a temporary hostel for visiting art enthusiasts. However, an unseasonable heatwave in the middle of the build leaves the team struggling to complete the construction in time to accommodate paying guests(n)

programme.title: Grime & Punishment: Dirty Britain

programme.desc: The pig and his farmyard pals take a trip to the big city, where their curiosity and love of adventure lands them in trouble, leaving the courageous Babe to find a way to save his chums from danger. Family comedy sequel, starring Magda Szubanski, Mickey Rooney and James Cromwell, and featuring the voice of Elizabeth Daily as Babe(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Part two of two. On the first hot day since their arrival in Barbados, the pals attend Ross's lecture, which is well received by his colleagues. Ross and Joey experience a change of fortunes, while Monica and Mike take part in a marathon ping-pong match, leaving Chandler irritated and Phoebe smitten(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Joey struggle to find the words to tell Ross about their kiss in Barbados, while Phoebe makes the alarming discovery that Mike has a girlfriend. Starring Jennifer Aniston, Matt LeBlanc and Lisa Kudrow(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to put on a brave face and invites Joey and Rachel to dinner with him and Charlie, but gets drunk and makes an embarrassing scene. Frank Jr tries to talk Phoebe into taking care of one of the triplets, while Chandler and Monica learn more about adoption(n)

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Disturbed by Kyle's behaviour, Roxy confides in Shane, who is not so forgiving and plans to let Kyle know. Elly struggles with a crying baby Aster, but Finn lends support(n)

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath(n)

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian returns to discover the big stories and untold tales behind Scotland's beloved spots, with privileged access to some of the country's most famous locations. In a warm, light-hearted but always informative journey, she looks in-depth at the history, characters and treasures hidden in these stunning spots. In the first episode, Susan explores the wilds of the Cairngorms. Covering an area twice the size of the Lake District, the Cairngorms is Britain's largest National Park, bursting with scenery, history and heritage(n)

programme.title: Holidaying with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer, with the help of a rotating cast of famous guests, travels the globe to sample dream holidays. On a sight-seeing safari tour on the Greek Island of Corfu, Jane enjoys a local liqueur at a kumquat distillery, before being whisked away on a speedboat tour of the western coast. Her first guest is TV presenter and former Strictly winner Ore Oduba, who indulges in a luxury three-day tour of the Lake District. Finally, former royal reporter Jennie Bond takes her best friend Sue on a fun trip to Portugal's vibrant capital, Lisbon(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay Comedy Genius: In His Own Words
programme.desc: A celebration of the life and career of the comedian, featuring interviews with the man himself as well as friends and colleagues Pete Waterman, Jenny Powell and Bobby Davro. This film charts the comic's rise from the back streets of Bolton to performing record-breaking arena tours and also included are some classic comedy routines, along with some rare gems and clips from hit TV series Phoenix Nights and Car Share(n)

programme.title: Puss in Boots (2011)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling feline joins forces with a cunning thief and a treacherous old friend to steal a magical golden goose from a giant's castle. He must save the day when it transpires that their actions have unleashed a terrible danger on his home town. Animated comedy spin-off from the Shrek movies, with the voices of Antonio Banderas and Salma Hayek(n)

programme.title: Rango (2011)
programme.desc: A pet chameleon with an active imagination ends up lost in the desert. He makes his way to a lawless town of animals, where he is mistaken for a deadly gunslinger and made sheriff. He organises the townsfolk into a posse when bandits led by an evil rattlesnake steal their supply of water. Oscar-winning animated comedy, with the voices of Johnny Depp, Isla Fisher and Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: A Dog's Purpose (2017)
programme.desc: A dog is reincarnated as different breeds over several generations, with multiple owners and with a variety of names, but he is fated to be reacquainted, decades later, with his original owner. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Quaid, with the voice of Josh Gad(n)

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee (1986)
programme.desc: A New York reporter searches the Australian Outback for a maverick hunter with a legendary reputation, and invites the adventurer back to America. But the die-hard Aussie needs to draw on all his survival skills to adjust to life in the urban jungle. Comedy adventure, written by and starring Paul Hogan, with Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Mark Blum(n)

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II (1988)
programme.desc: The rugged Australian adventurer sets out to save his reporter girlfriend from New York drug dealers. Having rescued her from their clutches, he realises they will soon be coming after him - so he decides to even the odds by luring them to the Outback for a final showdown. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Paul Hogan, Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Charles S Dutton(n)

programme.title: Hunt for the Wilderpeople (2016)
programme.desc: A 12-year-old brought up in the city is sent to be fostered by the good-natured Bella and her grumpy husband Hec, who live on a farm in rural New Zealand. When tragedy strikes. Ricky runs off into the wilderness, pursued by Hec - inadvertently sparking a national manhunt for the two of them. Comedy adventure written and directed by Taika Waititi, starring Sam Neill and Julian Dennison(n)

programme.title: Reservoir Dogs (1992)
programme.desc: Criminals are employed by a crime boss to carry out a diamond robbery that goes disastrously wrong. In the bloody aftermath of the heist, they try to establish what happened and how the police were aware of their plans all along - leading to the hardening suspicion that one of them betrayed the gang. Quentin Tarantino's thriller, starring Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Michael Madsen, Chris Penn and Steve Buscemi(n)

programme.title: Free Fire (2016)
programme.desc: It is 1978 and two Irish terrorists are in Boston to buy a shipment of assault rifles from a South African arms dealer, backed-up by hired muscle to help with a deal brokered by a woman and her psychopathic colleague. The deal takes place in a rundown warehouse and it is not long before a minor altercation gets out of hand and bullets start flying in every direction. Ben Wheatley's comedy crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Brie Larson, Sharlto Copley and Armie Hammer(n)

programme.title: Small Soldiers (1998)
programme.desc: A hi-tech computer chip has a bizarre effect on a squad of toy soldiers, causing mayhem for their owner as the miniature marauders engage in a series of scaled-down skirmishes. Children's adventure mixing live action and computer animation, starring Kirsten Dunst, Denis Leary and Gregory Smith, with the voices of Tommy Lee Jones, Frank Langella, Sarah Michelle Gellar and Christina Ricci(n)

programme.title: Endless Love (2014)
programme.desc: A girl from a wealthy family falls in love with a mysterious man over the course of the summer. However, their relationship is threatened by her parents' disapproval, and as he works to prove himself worthy of her, his own shady past is brought to light. Romantic drama based on Scott Spencer's novel, with Alex Pettyfer and Gabriella Wilde(n)

programme.title: Lemony Snicket's A Series of Unfortunate Events (2004)
programme.desc: Three orphans are entrusted into the care of their sinister uncle, a master of disguise who intends to kill them for the family fortune. Quickly realising he is up to no good, the children flee, moving from one prospective guardian to the next - but their wily relative is never far behind. Comedy adventure based on the children's books, starring Jim Carrey, Meryl Streep, Jude Law, Liam Aitken and Emily Browning, with Kara and Shelby Hoffman(n)

programme.title: Transformers: Age of Extinction (2014)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots are driven into hiding when the human race turns against them. When rediscovered by a kindly inventor and his daughter, they come out of exile to save the world, battling a new breed of human-created Transformers. These deadly new machines were created to hunt them down, but are now growing independent and intend to make use of technology from the Transformers' home planet in a plot that could threaten all life on Earth. Fourth film in the sci-fi adventure series, starring Mark Wahlberg, Stanley Tucci and Kelsey Grammer(n)

programme.title: Kingsman: The Secret Service (2015)
programme.desc: A streetwise teenager is taken under the wing of his dead father's old colleague, an agent working for a super-secret spy organisation. Will the youngster prove to be a chip off the old block by proving useful during a battle against a twisted, hi-tech genius? Action comedy based on a comic book, starring Colin Firth, Taron Egerton and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: Zoolander 2 (2016)
programme.desc: Dim-witted supermodels Derek Zoolander and Hansel McDonald must step back into the spotlight and save the day when a maniacal villain starts murdering some of the world's most beautiful pop stars. Comedy sequel, starring Ben Stiller and Owen Wilson, with Penelope Cruz, Will Ferrell and Kristen Wiig(n)

programme.title: Entertainment (2015)
programme.desc: A middle-aged comedian plays a string of dead-end shows in the Mojave desert en route to meeting his estranged daughter. Drama, starring Gregg Turkington, with John C Reilly, Tye Sheridan and Annabella Lwin(n)

programme.title: The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie (2004)
programme.desc: The plucky sea creature sets out to prove his boss Mr Krabs is innocent of stealing Neptune's crown. However, the missing item has ended up in sinister Shell City, which all sea-dwellers fear and from which few have ever returned. Animated comedy, with the voices of Tom Kenny, Bill Fagerbakke, Clancy Brown and Scarlett Johansson, plus a cameo appearance by David Hasselhoff(n)

programme.title: Kubo and the Two Strings (2016)
programme.desc: A boy unwittingly summons an evil spirit to wreak havoc on his peaceful village home. The only hope of defeating the vengeful creature is to find a magical suit of armour belonging to the boy's father - but finding it will entail matching wits with an assortment of monstrous foes. Animated adventure, with the voices of Charlize Theron, Art Parkinson and Matthew McConaughey(n)

programme.title: Flushed Away (2006)
programme.desc: A pampered pet rodent is flushed down a toilet and ends up in a vast underground rat city in the sewers of London. He is befriended by a quick-witted rat who offers to help him find his way home, but first the hapless pair have to escape the clutches of an evil toad bent on ridding the rodent metropolis of its residents. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Kate Winslet, Ian McKellen, Shane Richie, Jean Reno and Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters (2013)
programme.desc: The modern-day teenage son of the Greek god Poseidon must lead his friends on a perilous journey into the Bermuda triangle. They need to recover the legendary Golden Fleece to save the world, but getting to it means braving all manner of terrifying monsters. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Logan Lerman and Alexandra Daddario(n)

programme.title: Project Almanac (2015)
programme.desc: Following the death of his inventor father, a teenager finds plans for a time-travel device. He and his friends decide to build it, and together they embark on an amazing adventure back in time - but soon discover the perils of altering history. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jonny Weston, Sofia Black-D'Elia and Sam Lerner(n)

programme.title: Transformers: The Last Knight (2017)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots and their human allies face a confrontation with their original creator, who has brainwashed their leader and intends to save her own home planet by destroying Earth. To save the human race they must recover an ancient artefact from Arthurian legend - but their mission is threatened by old enemies as they uncover a terrible secret about the history of the world. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg, Anthony Hopkins, Gemma Chan, Stanley Tucci and John Turturro(n)

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: Jimmy convinces Maya to go on a night out together on New Year's Eve. Meanwhile, Dylan spends the evening trying to win the new family dog's affection(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry is excited when a modelling agency representative approaches him, but Erica thinks it's a scam. Meanwhile, Adam tries to get Murray to build bridges with his father(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys have fun at the expense of Penny's boyfriend Zack, but later try to make amends by enlisting his help with the New Year's costume competition at the comic-book store. Comedy, starring Kaley Cuoco and Simon Helberg(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Internet prankster Stevo The Madman plans a Caribbean-themed Christmas dinner party, hoping to impress his rivals with authentic Caribbean cuisine and entertainment(n)

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Marshall and Lily decide to put their argument about Marshall accepting a judgeship without discussing it on hold so they can enjoy their reunion. Meanwhile, Robin and Ted finally discover what Barney does for a living when they cross-examine him after he has had too much to drink. American sitcom, starring Josh Radnor and Alyson Hannigan(n)

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: In a series of flashbacks celebrating the show's 200th episode, it is revealed how Ted first met his future wife, and how they share a history of missed connections, especially after he was introduced to her room-mate Cindy (Rachel Bilson). The couple realise they have had many of the same friends and acquaintances over the years. American sitcom, starring Josh Radnor and Alyson Hannigan(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon leaves for Texas when his sister goes into labour. While he is away, the rest of the friends decorate for Christmas and reflect on how much he has changed their lives. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny makes an important decision after Leonard tries to help her in the aftermath of a failed role on a TV show. Meanwhile, Raj and Stuart practise talking to strangers at a shopping centre, and Sheldon studies to become a comic genius. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube

programme.title: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
programme.desc: Animated adventure, based on the popular children's bedtime book by Michael Rosen and Helen Oxenbury. Stan, Katie, Rosie, Max and the baby, along with Rufus the dog, all embark on a quest to find some bears. Trekking through whirling snowstorms, muddy bogs and dark forests, the siblings refuse to let anything get in their way. However, when Rosie and Rufus end up getting lost, bear-hunting suddenly doesn't seem quite so much fun after all. Featuring the voices of Olivia Colman, Pam Ferris and Mark Williams as Mum, Grandma and Dad respectively(n)

Family comedy sequel, starring Zachary Gordon and Steve Zahn(n)
